summary.data_source: NCDOT non-motorist crash data, Durham County (2019-2024)

findings: Baseline inequity: Black residents are 32% of Durham's population but 47% of pedestrian and cyclist crash victims — before AI enters the picture (NCDOT, 2019–2023)
AI compounds this: crash prediction recall is 29% in the poorest quintile vs 67% in the richest — a 38-point gap that misdirects safety investment away from communities already at greatest risk
Ridge regression trained on real 2019–2022 NCDOT non-motorist data with Census ACS demographic features, evaluated on 2023 crashes
Correcting the model's allocation would shift $1.3M toward the lowest-income quintile per $5M safety cycle

racial_baseline.description: Pre-AI baseline: existing racial disparity in who bears crash risk in Durham, before any AI allocation decisions
racial_baseline.source: NCDOT non-motorist crash records 2019-2023 + Census ACS 2024
